head: df3e1a1
purpose: Short machine-readable current context and routing for agents. This file is not project history.

canonical_read_order: relevant local AGENTS.md for touched package/app
one task-specific active doc when scope requires it
read_policy.history_read_condition: Read logs/archive only when explicitly asked for history, repeated-error debugging, regression archaeology, or old audit details.

current_context.worktree: dirty: multiple documentation, deploy, loadtest and admin/operator changes are in progress
current_context.active_branch_role: serega is the current integration/development branch
current_context.dev_contour: dev-deploy branch plus DEV VPS, DEV Cloudflare tunnel, DEV VK group and DEV payment/provider secrets. DEV should mirror production architecture with separate secrets and routes.
current_context.prod_contour: main only. Production deploy goes through GitHub Actions, GHCR images, VPS deploy script, smoke checks and rollback policy.
current_context.loadtest_contour: APP_ENV=loadtest with mock providers, mock payments and mock delivery only. Production hosts and paid providers are blocked for load tests.
current_context.current_doc_work: Canonical documentation cleanup is active. Keep AGENTS.md, docs/INDEX.md, RUNBOOK.md/runbooks and this state file concise.

recent_decisions: Default context is intentionally small: AGENTS.md, .agents/state.json and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
docs/HANDOFF_CURRENT.md is the only active handoff slot; completed handoffs live under docs/archive/handoffs/.
RUNBOOK.md is an entrypoint; task details live under docs/runbooks/.
DEV and PROD envs must be compared by variable names without printing values.
DEV is the daily integration/testing contour; PROD secrets must not be copied from DEV.
Provider calls remain isolated in workers/adapters; VK bot, Mini App and cmd/api must not call AI providers directly.
Billing remains ledger-based and payment top-ups require provider-verified webhook/reconciliation.
active_priorities: Finish documentation canonicalization without turning docs into history logs.
Keep DEV and PROD deploy workflows aligned but isolated by secrets, tunnel, VK group and payment settings.
Run longer sustained load tests before claiming scale readiness.
Verify live YooKassa smoke before real-money confidence: payment.succeeded, payment.canceled, refund.succeeded, replay/idempotency and reconciliation.
Plan operational migration to external/managed Postgres, Redis and S3 before serious traffic.
Use protected PR/review flow for main; no force push.
open_risks: DEV/PROD env drift is still possible if the parity allowlist becomes too broad or secret files are edited manually.
Real provider/payment smoke tests can spend money or touch external systems; require explicit approval and correct environment.
Mock load tests do not prove real provider quota or external API throughput.
Lot/FIFO attribution is still needed before automatic or partial refunds for already-spent credits.
Old archived handoffs may contain stale branch names or outdated priorities; do not treat them as current truth.
security_invariants: No secrets, tokens, auth headers, full launch params, prompt bodies, provider payloads, private artifact URLs or raw PII in git, logs, docs or chat.
VK handlers, Mini App BFF and cmd/api never call AI providers directly.
Billing is append-only ledger based; no direct balance mutation.
Payment redirect/confirmation URL is not proof of payment.
YooKassa top-up credits only through provider-verified webhook/reconciliation plus ledger.
Do not weaken auth, signature checks, moderation, billing, idempotency, TLS, webhook trust or production CORS.

subagent_policy.output_schema: status, findings file:line issue fix, changed_files/tests, residual_risks
